DELETE_args.description: Delete all workloads associated with the dataset.

limit.description: Return no more than this many results at once (see resume).

resume.description: Continue returning results from previous call using this token (token should come from the previous call, resume cannot be used with other options).

sort.description: The field that will be used for sorting.

resource_description: This resource represents the collection of "workloads" selected for the dataset specified in the URI. A workload is a selection of specific values for each metric in its dataset. These values are "pinned"; statistics for them will always be collected and available for viewing at /statistics/current?key=node.accounting.workloads.dataset.<dataset-id>, even if they are not among the top resource-consuming combinations of their metrics. For example, a dataset with the metrics "zone_name" and "protocol" might have a workload with the selection "zone_name:System", "protocol:nfs4". This configuration means statistics about nfs v4 usage in the System zone are "pinned" and will always be collected and available, in addition to statistics about the top resource-consuming zone_name/protocol combinations.
